domain: Khmer Angkor baray hydraulic cycle (4 reservoirs, ~100 million m³ combined; wet-season collection + dry-season distribution via canal network; phase-locked to monsoon climate)
specific_mechanism: Two-phase synchronized cycle: wet-phase ingest, dry-phase output, reservoir buffers between.
llm_application: BBPLTC: wet phase = high-traffic window, gradients collected to baray reservoir (large slow-evicting gradient memory) without weight update; dry phase = off-peak, reservoir distributed to model via batched fine-tuning. Phase-synchronized with deployment traffic patterns.
novelty_claim: Mid-Training (2510.06826), Periodic Asynchrony (2511.18871), LR-Decay-Free (2603.16127) cover schedule research. None retrieve traffic-synced wet-collect/dry-distribute baray-buffered continual-training cycle.
